mission.title: Install and Configure Kgateway on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kgateway is a cloud-native API gateway designed for microservices, providing a resilient and performance-oriented control plane that implements the Kubernetes Gateway API. Installing kgateway allows you to manage API connectivity and apply policies like authentication and rate limiting seamlessly across your services.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for kgateway
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for kgateway to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Apply the kgateway Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description: Apply the necessary CRDs for kgateway using the following command.

steps.title: Deploy the kgateway Controller
steps.description: Deploy the kgateway controller to manage the API gateway functionality.

steps.title: Deploy the kgateway Gateway
steps.description: Deploy the kgateway Gateway resource to start managing your API traffic.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check the status of the kgateway pods to ensure they are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the kgateway pods running in the kgateway namespace. You can verify this by running `kubectl get pods --namespace kgateway` and seeing the pods in a 'Running' state. Additionally, you will have configured a BackendConfigPolicy for your services.

uninstall.title: Remove the kgateway Gateway
uninstall.description: Delete the kgateway Gateway resource to stop managing API traffic.

uninstall.title: Remove the kgateway CRDs
uninstall.description: Delete the kgateway CRDs to clean up the resources.

uninstall.title: Delete the kgateway Namespace
uninstall.description: Remove the kgateway namespace if it is no longer needed.

upgrade.title: Upgrade kgateway
upgrade.description: To upgrade kgateway, reapply the latest manifests from the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the kgateway pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors using:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kgateway
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs Not Found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues with CRDs, ensure they are applied correctly. Reapply the CRDs with:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kgateway-dev/kgateway/main/deploy/crds.yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Service Connectivity Issues
troubleshooting.description: If there are issues with service connectivity, verify that the services are correctly defined and that the kgateway Gateway resource is configured properly.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed to manage your cluster.
